mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals aggregate labor income expansion, which drives consumption and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:payrolls lead household consumption and aggregate demand
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with positive PAYEMS growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, unconventional monetary policy expectations become dominant, compressing long-term yields and boosting equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:effective federal funds rate near zero lower bound in early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate a coordinated demand recovery across consumption and investment sectors, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days than either proxy alone.
failure_condition: In periods where both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary periods where the unemployment rate exceeds 6 percent, a month-over-month increase in unemployment signals accelerating labor market deterioration and collapsing aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:unemployment rate climbed above 6% during 2008-2009 recession
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6%, month-over-month rises in UNRATE are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered months over a 10-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, supporting corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month decreases in the federal funds rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls generate a synergistic stimulus effect where monetary easing reinforces labor income growth, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: coordinated monetary easing and labor market recovery
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS exhibits a t-statistic less than 1.5 in predicting 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 percent signals accelerating inflation that raises the real cost of capital and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL increasing more than 0.3% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals a broad-based expansion in aggregate demand that lifts corporate top-line revenues and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations over a 5-year rolling window.
